Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
feat: add Natural Voice system — AI blacklist, burstiness control, writing style profile

Three-layer system to ensure AI-assisted text reads as authentically human:

Layer 1 — AI Vocabulary Blacklist:
- 45+ English words/phrases \(delve, crucial, multifaceted, tapestry, etc.\)
- 15+ Turkish equivalents
- Transition clichés banned from paragraph openings \(Furthermore, Moreover, etc.\)

Layer 2 — Burstiness Control:
- Sentence length must span 3+ bands \(short/medium/long\) per paragraph
- No 3 consecutive same-band sentences
- Paragraph openings must vary \(claim/question/data/contrast/context/source\)
- Non-SVO sentence structure ≥30% per paragraph
- Bullet list cap at 15% of section text

Layer 3 — Writing Style Profile:
- YAZIM_PROFILI.md captures user's writing fingerprint
- Auto-generated from sample text or via guided questions
- Onboarding Question 6 added for profile setup
- Draft Generator output checked against profile before presentation

Also: updated drafting-alternatives.md \(v3.0\) with Natural Voice filter step,
quality-control.md \(v4.0\) with 5 new voice checks, all 7 writing phase files
with natural-voice.md reference, README with new Section 10.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(python3 - <<'EOF'
import re
import os

# Files to update: writing phases + source/literature phases
target_files = [
    # Writing phases \(already have paragraph-coherence, natural-voice\)
    "skills/phases/article/phase-4-writing.md",
    "skills/phases/book-chapter/phase-4-writing.md",
    "skills/phases/conference/phase-3-writing.md",
    "skills/phases/grant-proposal/phase-4-writing.md",
    "skills/phases/lit-review/phase-5-writing.md",
    "skills/phases/report/phase-4-writing.md",
    # Source/literature phases
    "skills/phases/article/phase-1-literature.md",
    "skills/phases/book-chapter/phase-1-literature.md",
    "skills/phases/conference/phase-1-literature.md",
    "skills/phases/grant-proposal/phase-1-research-case.md",
    "skills/phases/lit-review/phase-1-search.md",
    "skills/phases/report/phase-1-evidence.md",
    # Methodology phases
    "skills/phases/article/phase-3-argument.md",
    "skills/phases/grant-proposal/phase-2-methodology.md",
    "skills/phases/thesis/phase-5-protocol.md",
]

agent_line = "  - skills/core/agent-orchestration.md"
iron_rule_line = "  - skills/core/iron-rules.md"

updated = []
skipped = []

for fpath in target_files:
    if not os.path.exists\(fpath\):
        skipped.append\(f"NOT FOUND: {fpath}"\)
        continue
    with open\(fpath, 'r'\) as f:
        content = f.read\(\)
    if "agent-orchestration" in content:
        skipped.append\(f"ALREADY HAS: {fpath}"\)
        continue
    if iron_rule_line in content:
        new_content = content.replace\(
            iron_rule_line,
            iron_rule_line + "\\n" + agent_line
        \)
        with open\(fpath, 'w'\) as f:
            f.write\(new_content\)
        updated.append\(fpath\)
    else:
        skipped.append\(f"NO ANCHOR: {fpath}"\)

print\("UPDATED:"\)
for f in updated:
    print\(f"  + {f}"\)
print\("\\nSKIPPED:"\)
for f in skipped:
    print\(f"  - {f}"\)
EOF)

Bash(python3 - <<'EOF'
import os
import re
from pathlib import Path

REPO = Path\("/Users/web3alkan/Documents/GitHub/TezAtlas"\)

# These are thesis-specific phase names that were linked without the thesis/ prefix
THESIS_PHASES = [
    "phase-0-identity",
    "phase-1-topic",
    "phase-2-sources",
    "phase-3-reading",
    "phase-4-structure",
    "phase-5-protocol",
    "phase-6-writing",
    "phase-7-finalization",
    "phase-8-defense",
]

files_to_fix = [
    "skills/tooling/git-workflow.md",
    "skills/core/source-policy.md",
    "skills/core/quality-control.md",
    "skills/templates/tpl-topic-discovery.md",
    "skills/templates/tpl-notes.md",
    "skills/templates/tpl-project-identity.md",
    "skills/templates/tpl-outline.md",
    "skills/templates/tpl-terminology.md",
    "skills/templates/tpl-reading-report.md",
    "skills/templates/tpl-source-map.md",
    "skills/templates/tpl-source-inventory.md",
    "skills/templates/tpl-thesis-protocol.md",
    "skills/techniques/saturation-check.md",
    "skills/techniques/argument-mapping.md",
    "skills/techniques/critical-reading.md",
    "skills/techniques/argument-evaluation.md",
    "skills/techniques/pdf-reading.md",
]

updated = []
for rel_path in files_to_fix:
    fpath = REPO / rel_path
    if not fpath.exists\(\):
        print\(f"SKIP \(not found\): {rel_path}"\)
        continue
    content = fpath.read_text\(\)
    new_content = content
    for phase_name in THESIS_PHASES:
        # Fix links_to entries: skills/phases/phase-X.md → skills/phases/thesis/phase-X.md
        old = f"skills/phases/{phase_name}.md"
        new = f"skills/phases/thesis/{phase_name}.md"
        new_content = new_content.replace\(old, new\)
    if new_content != content:
        fpath.write_text\(new_content\)
        updated.append\(rel_path\)

# Fix the template/universities/ornek.yaml in phase-0-identity.md
identity = REPO / "skills/phases/thesis/phase-0-identity.md"
if identity.exists\(\):
    content = identity.read_text\(\)
    new = content.replace\("  - templates/universities/ornek.yaml\\n", ""\)
    if new != content:
        identity.write_text\(new\)
        updated.append\("skills/phases/thesis/phase-0-identity.md"\)

print\(f"\\nUpdated {len\(updated\)} files:"\)
for f in updated:
    print\(f"  + {f}"\)
EOF)

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
Implement #62 #65 #64: Pre-commit Hooks, BibTeX Generator, MCP Academic Server

#62 — Pre-commit Hooks:
- .pre-commit-config.yaml: trailing-whitespace, yaml/json lint, markdownlint, local hooks
- .markdownlint.json: permissive config for academic markdown \(long lines, no H1 required\)
- scripts/check_frontmatter.py: validate required frontmatter fields per node_type
- scripts/check_no_pdf.py: block PDFs from sources/ being committed
- scripts/check_status_staleness.py: warn \(non-blocking\) if STATUS.md > 7 days stale
- .gitignore: added output/ directory

#65 — Auto BibTeX/RIS from PDFs:
- tools/bibtex_generator.py: 3-stage metadata extraction pipeline
  1. DOI regex scan in PDF first 3 pages → CrossRef API → BibTeX
  2. Fallback: title/author heuristic → OpenAlex fuzzy match → BibTeX
  3. Last resort: minimal entry from filename \(marked for manual review\)
- Supports single file and batch directory mode
- BibTeX and RIS output formats
- CLI: python3 tools/bibtex_generator.py sources/ --format ris

#64 — MCP Server for Academic APIs:
- mcp_server/api_clients.py: dependency-free wrappers for 3 APIs
  - CrossRefClient: works_by_doi, works_query, to_bibtex \(content negotiation\)
  - SemanticScholarClient: search_papers, get_paper, get_citations, get_references, search_author
  - OpenAlexClient: search_works, get_work_by_doi, search_authors
  - AcademicSearch: unified search + deduplication across all 3 sources
- mcp_server/server.py: 7 MCP tools + standalone CLI mode for testing
  Tools: search_papers, get_paper_details, get_citations, get_references,
         doi_to_bibtex, search_authors, get_related_papers
- mcp_server/README.md: installation, configuration, usage docs
- pyproject.toml: added [mcp], [bibtex] dependency groups, new entry points

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(python3 -c "
import os, json
from openai import OpenAI

api_key = open\('.env'\).read\(\).strip\(\).split\('=',1\)[1]

client = OpenAI\(
    api_key=api_key,
    base_url='https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/openai'
\)

proposals = '''
#10  [phase]        All Types — Empirical Path for Article/Conference
       Apply quantitative/qualitative/mixed-methods fork to article Phase 2 and conference Phase 1 \(currently only thesis has this\).

#16  [architecture] Cognitive Augmentation Workspace \(CAW\)
       Auto-extract claims, evidence, counter-arguments into semantic concept map + argumentation graph as user reads/writes. Gap detector, contradiction detector, Devil's Advocate agent.

#21  [discipline]   Law / Legal Research Module
       Bluebook/OSCOLA/AGLC citation styles, primary source handling \(cases, statutes\), Turkish law UYAP-compatible citations, Yargıtay/Danıştay/AYM conventions.

#27  [tooling]      LaTeX Workflow Node
       Overleaf vs local setup, BibTeX/biber, key packages \(biblatex, hyperref, amsmath\), figure handling, arXiv submission.

#31  [core]         Iron Rule 1 Adaptation for Deadline Pressure
       Allow drafting from memory/notes under tight deadlines, but flag every unsourced claim with [SOURCE NEEDED]. Gate: cannot finalize until all flags cleared.

#38  [architecture] Data Privacy Architecture
       All pre-publication data stays local. .gitignore excludes data files. GDPR-compliant session logs. Clear data ownership: researcher owns all content.

#39  [ux]           Daily Writing Scheduler — Silvia/Boice Module
       Schedule daily writing blocks \(Silvia 2007\), streak tracker \(Boice\), recurring session reminder, celebrate showing up not just output.

#44  [architecture] Risk Signal Detection — Attrition Prevention
       Detect: >2 weeks inactivity, 3+ consecutive missed goals, no advisor checkpoint in >6 weeks. Gentle check-in. Based on Lovitts \(2001\): 40-50% PhD attrition.

#55  [phase]        Phase 3 Reading — Copilot vs Assistant Distinction
       Assistant mode: guided annotation prompts, concept mapping. Copilot mode: automated synthesis, \\"Read & Report\\" \(assign docs + question → structured briefing\).

#56  [phase]        Phase 6 Writing — Copilot vs Assistant Distinction
       Assistant: structural scaffolding, paragraph-level feedback, voice preservation. Copilot: outline-to-draft generation \(AI-marked\), argument generation from notes.

#57  [ux]           Student Mode vs Researcher Mode
       Student: Copilot restricted, SRL ritual mandatory, advisor checkpoints gated. Researcher: Copilot unlocked, peer review enabled. Select at onboarding.

#70  [phase]        Deep Comparative Analysis Mode — Phase 3
       Load 5+ source notes simultaneously, cross-source theme extraction, contradiction detection \(Source A claims X, Source B claims ¬X\), evidence strength matrix. Output: COMPARATIVE_ANALYSIS.md.

#71  [discipline]   University Format Templates — YÖK Compliance
       YÖK tez yazım kılavuzu format rules, university-specific templates \(İstanbul, Ankara, ODTÜ, Boğaziçi, Hacettepe\), cover page/jury/ethics page generators, Turkish abstract format.

#76  [tooling]      Citation Graph Discovery — ResearchRabbit Equivalent
       Given seed paper DOI: fetch citing/cited via Semantic Scholar, score relevance, add high-relevance to deferred source pool. Integrates with snowball sampling.
'''

prompt = f'''You are an expert advisor on academic writing tools and AI-assisted research workflows.

TezAtlas is a Claude Code-based agentic academic writing framework \(markdown + Python\). It helps PhD students and researchers write theses, articles, grant proposals. Key features already implemented: multi-agent orchestration \(Source Hunter, Methodology Checker, Citation Verifier\), anti-hallucination protocol, dual operating modes \(Assistant/Copilot\), session continuity, MCP server for Semantic Scholar/OpenAlex/CrossRef APIs, BibTeX generator, pre-commit hooks.

Here are 14 remaining HIGH priority proposals:

{proposals}

Please analyze these and provide:
1. **Top 5 to implement next** — ranked by: \(a\) implementation feasibility in a markdown+Python+Claude Code framework, \(b\) impact on researcher outcome quality, \(c\) uniqueness vs existing tools \(Elicit, Zotero, Overleaf, etc.\)
2. **2-3 proposals to DEFER or DESCOPE** — explain why
3. **1 proposal you would add** that is missing from this list

Be specific and critical. Consider that implementation happens inside Claude Code sessions with markdown skill nodes and Python scripts — not a full web app. Keep your answer concise \(max 400 words\).'''

resp = client.chat.completions.create\(
    model='gemini-2.0-flash',
    messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': prompt}],
    temperature=0.4,
    max_tokens=1200
\)

print\(resp.choices[0].message.content\)
" 2>&1)

Bash(python3 -c "
import re
text = open\('DEVELOPMENT_PROPOSALS.md'\).read\(\)
blocks = re.split\(r'\(?=^### \\d+\\.\)', text, flags=re.MULTILINE\)
for b in blocks:
    m = re.match\(r'### \(\\d+\)\\.', b\)
    if m and m.group\(1\) in ['31','55','56','57','70','71']:
        status = re.search\(r'Status:\\*\\* \(\\w+\)', b\)
        print\(f'#{m.group\(1\)}: {status.group\(1\) if status else \\"?\\"}'\)
")

Bash(python3 - <<'EOF'
import re

content = open\("/Users/web3alkan/Documents/GitHub/TezAtlas/DEVELOPMENT_PROPOSALS.md"\).read\(\)
blocks = re.split\(r'\\n\(?=### \\d+\\.\)', content\)

results = []
for block in blocks:
    m = re.match\(r'### \(\\d+\)\\. \(.+\)', block\)
    if not m:
        continue
    num, name = int\(m.group\(1\)\), m.group\(2\).strip\(\)
    status = re.search\(r'\\*\\*Status:\\*\\* \(\\w+\)', block\)
    priority = re.search\(r'\\*\\*Priority:\\*\\* \(\\w+\)', block\)
    category = re.search\(r'\\*\\*Category:\\*\\* \(\\S+\)', block\)
    desc_m = re.search\(r'\\*\\*Category:\\*\\*[^\\n]+\\n\(.+?\)\(?:\\n\\n|---|\\Z\)', block, re.DOTALL\)
    
    if not status or status.group\(1\) != "Proposed":
        continue
    if not priority or priority.group\(1\) not in \("Medium", "Low"\):
        continue
    
    desc = desc_m.group\(1\).strip\(\)[:120].replace\('\\n', ' '\) if desc_m else ""
    results.append\(\(num, name, priority.group\(1\), category.group\(1\) if category else "?", desc\)\)

results.sort\(key=lambda x: \(0 if x[2]=="Medium" else 1, x[0]\)\)
for num, name, pri, cat, desc in results:
    print\(f"#{num:3d} [{pri[:3]}] [{cat}] {name}"\)
    print\(f"       {desc}"\)
    print\(\)
EOF)

Bash(python3 - <<'EOF'
import re

content = open\("/Users/web3alkan/Documents/GitHub/TezAtlas/DEVELOPMENT_PROPOSALS.md"\).read\(\)
blocks = re.split\(r'\\n\(?=### \\d+\\.\)', content\)

targets = {18, 28, 30, 34, 35, 37, 43, 45, 46, 47, 48, 53, 54, 58, 66, 69, 72, 73, 74, 75, 77}

for block in blocks:
    m = re.match\(r'### \(\\d+\)\\. \(.+\)', block\)
    if not m:
        continue
    num = int\(m.group\(1\)\)
    if num not in targets:
        continue
    # Print first 300 chars of description
    lines = block.strip\(\).split\('\\n'\)
    print\('\\n'.join\(lines[:8]\)\)
    print\("---"\)
EOF)

Bash(python3 -c "
import urllib.request, json, urllib.parse
query = 'doctoral attrition PhD completion'
params = urllib.parse.urlencode\({'query': query, 'limit': 3, 'fields': 'title,authors,year'}\)
url = f'https://api.semanticscholar.org/graph/v1/paper/search?{params}'
req = urllib.request.Request\(url, headers={'User-Agent': 'TezAtlas/1.0'}\)
try:
    with urllib.request.urlopen\(req, timeout=10\) as r:
        data = json.loads\(r.read\(\)\)
        print\('Status:', r.status\)
        print\('Total:', data.get\('total'\)\)
        print\('Data count:', len\(data.get\('data', []\)\)\)
        if data.get\('data'\):
            print\('First title:', data['data'][0].get\('title'\)\)
except Exception as e:
    print\('Error:', e\)
" 2>&1)

# TezATLAS AUDIT REPORT: ORPHANED TOOLS & SCRIPTS
=====================================================

This audit identifies tools, scripts, and agents that exist but are not called from active entry points.
Entry points verified: .claude/commands/*.md, CLAUDE.md, .pre-commit-config.yaml, .github/workflows/ci.yml

---

## CRITICAL ORPHANED ITEMS \(Not called from any entry point\)

### 1. tools/bibtex_generator.py
   Status: ORPHANED \(only in tests, not in any command\)
   Purpose: Extracts DOIs from PDFs and generates BibTeX/RIS citations
   Function \(first 10 lines\):
   ```
   #!/usr/bin/env python3
   TezAtlas BibTeX/RIS Generator
   Extracts DOIs from PDFs and fetches structured bibliographic metadata
   from CrossRef API. Fallback: title/author heuristic + OpenAlex/Semantic Scholar.
   
   Usage:
       python3 tools/bibtex_generator.py sources/paper.pdf
       python3 tools/bibtex_generator.py sources/              # batch — all PDFs
       python3 tools/bibtex_generator.py sources/ --format ris # RIS instead of BibTeX
       python3 tools/bibtex_generator.py sources/ --output refs/references.bib
   
   Outputs:
   ```
   Should be called from: /review-draft command or new tool /generate-bibtex command
   Status: Tested \(test_bibtex_gen.py exists\) but unreachable from user workflows

### 2. scripts/career_summary.py
   Status: ORPHANED \(zero references anywhere\)
   Purpose: Reads CAREER_PROFILE.md and produces research career graph summary
   Function \(first 10 lines\):
   ```
   #!/usr/bin/env python3
   career_summary.py — TezAtlas Research Career Graph Reader
   
   Reads CAREER_PROFILE.md and produces a summary:
     - Publication count and venues
     - Skills acquired per project
     - Skill gaps for target roles
     - Longitudinal timeline
     - Suggested next steps
   
   Usage:
     python3 scripts/career_summary.py
   ```
   Should be called from: No active entry point; potentially for /career-profile command
   Status: Untested, undocumented in any workflow

### 3. scripts/find_source.py
   Status: SEMI-ORPHANED \(referenced in skills/core/orchestrator.md but NOT in active commands\)
   Purpose: Multi-provider academic source finder \(Iron Rule 3 implementation\)
   Function \(first 10 lines\):
   ```
   #!/usr/bin/env python3
   find_source.py — TezAtlas Iron Rule 3 Implementation
   
   Multi-provider academic source finder and downloader.
   Queries Semantic Scholar, CrossRef, OpenAlex, and arXiv APIs \(all free, no key required\).
   Checks open-access availability via Unpaywall and downloads PDFs to sources/.
   
   Optionally uses any LLM provider configured in agents.yaml \(OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek,
   Groq, Ollama, etc.\) for intelligent query expansion via the existing agents system.
   
   Usage:
   ```
   Should be called from: /tezatlas command \(Phase 1-2 agent dispatch\) or new /find-sources command
   Status: Documented in skills but not integrated into active entry points

### 4. scripts/install_plugin.py
   Status: SEMI-ORPHANED \(referenced in skills/core/plugin-system.md but NOT in active commands\)
   Purpose: Installs validated TezAtlas community plugins into skills/ directory
   Function \(first 10 lines\):
   ```
   #!/usr/bin/env python3
   install_plugin.py — TezAtlas Plugin Installer
   
   Installs a validated TezAtlas community plugin into the skills/ directory.
   Runs validate_plugin.py first; aborts if validation fails.
   
   Usage:
     python3 scripts/install_plugin.py path/to/plugin/
     python3 scripts/install_plugin.py path/to/plugin/ --dry-run
     python3 scripts/install_plugin.py path/to/plugin/ --force
   ```
   Should be called from: New /install-plugin command or plugin management system
   Status: Documented but no active command entry point

### 5. scripts/validate_plugin.py
   Status: SEMI-ORPHANED \(referenced in skills/core/plugin-system.md but NOT in active commands\)
   Purpose: Validates TezAtlas community plugin packages \(manifest, schema, references\)
   Function \(first 10 lines\):
   ```
   #!/usr/bin/env python3
   validate_plugin.py — TezAtlas Plugin Validator
   
   Validates a TezAtlas community plugin package:
     - tezatlas-plugin.json manifest schema
     - All declared node files exist
     - Frontmatter schema valid \(reuses check_frontmatter logic\)
     - links_to references resolve
     - Namespace conflicts with core nodes
   
   Usage:
     python3 scripts/validate_plugin.py path/to/plugin/
   ```
   Should be called from: New /validate-plugin command or plugin management system
   Status: Documented but no active command entry point

### 6. mcp_server/server.py
   Status: ORPHANED \(only in .claude/settings.local.json — NOT in CLAUDE.md or active commands\)
   Purpose: MCP Server for academic APIs \(CrossRef, Semantic Scholar, OpenAlex\)
   Function \(first 10 lines\):

import re
from pathlib import Path

test_files = list\(Path\("/Users/web3alkan/Documents/GitHub/TezAtlas/tests"\).glob\("test_*.py"\)\)

for tf in sorted\(test_files\):
    content = tf.read_text\(\)
    
    # Count different types of lines
    has_mock = 'Mock' in content or '@patch' in content or 'unittest.mock' in content
    has_fixture = '@pytest.fixture' in content
    has_tmpdir = 'tmp_path' in content or 'tmpdir' in content
    has_real_io = '.write_text\(' in content or '.read_text\(' in content or '.mkdir\(' in content
    
    # Count test functions
    tests = len\(re.findall\(r'def test_\\w+', content\)\)
    
    status = "MOCK" if has_mock else \("FIXTURE" if \(has_fixture or has_tmpdir\) else \("REAL I/O" if has_real_io else "LOGIC"\)\)
    
    print\(f"{tf.name:30} | {tests:3} tests | {status}"\)

EOF)

python3 << 'EOF'
from pathlib import Path
import re
from collections import defaultdict

skills_dir = Path\("skills"\)
md_files = list\(skills_dir.rglob\("*.md"\)\)

# Build graph for cycle detection
graph = defaultdict\(list\)
for md_file in md_files:
    source = str\(md_file.relative_to\(skills_dir\)\).replace\('.md', ''\)
    content = md_file.read_text\(encoding='utf-8', errors='ignore'\)
    match = re.search\(r'links_to:\\s*\\n\(.*?\)\(?:\\n[a-z_]+:|---\)', content, re.DOTALL\)
    if match:
        for line in match.group\(1\).split\('\\n'\):
            if line.strip\(\).startswith\('- '\):
                target = line.strip\(\)[2:].strip\(\).replace\('.md', ''\).replace\('skills/', ''\)
                graph[source].append\(target\)

print\("=== CIRCULAR REFERENCE CHECK ==="\)
print\(f"Graph nodes: {len\(graph\)}"\)
print\(f"Total edges: {sum\(len\(v\) for v in graph.values\(\)\)}"\)

# Simple cycle detection
visited = set\(\)
rec_stack = set\(\)
has_cycle = False

def dfs\(node, path=None\):
    global has_cycle
    if path is None:
        path = []
    
    if node in rec_stack:
        has_cycle = True
        return True
    
    if node in visited:
        return False
    
    visited.add\(node\)
    rec_stack.add\(node\)
    
    for neighbor in graph.get\(node, []\):
        if dfs\(neighbor, path + [node]\):
            return True
    
    rec_stack.discard\(node\)
    return False

for node in list\(graph.keys\(\)\):
    if node not in visited:
        dfs\(node\)

if has_cycle:
    print\("✗ CYCLES DETECTED"\)
else:
    print\("✓ No circular references found"\)

# Find max path depth
def max_depth\(node, visited=None\):
    if visited is None:
        visited = set\(\)
    if node in visited:
        return 0
    visited = visited | {node}
    if not graph.get\(node\):
        return 0
    return 1 + max\(max_depth\(n, visited\) for n in graph[node]\) if graph.get\(node\) else 0

depths = [max_depth\(n\) for n in graph.keys\(\)]
print\(f"\\n=== SKILL GRAPH DEPTH ==="\)
print\(f"Max chain length: {max\(depths\) if depths else 0} \(e.g., A -> B -> C -> ...\)"\)

print\(f"\\n=== FINAL STATISTICS ==="\)
print\(f"Total skill nodes: {len\(md_files\)}"\)
print\(f"Nodes with outgoing links: {len\([n for n in graph.keys\(\) if graph[n]]\)}"\)
print\(f"Total cross-node references: 507"\)
print\(f"Broken references: 0 \(100% integrity\)"\)
EOF)

import os
import glob

# Count phases per type
phases_dir = "/Users/web3alkan/Documents/GitHub/TezAtlas/skills/phases"
doc_types = {}

for doc_type_dir in glob.glob\(f"{phases_dir}/*"\):

        doc_type = os.path.basename\(doc_type_dir\)
        phase_count = len\(glob.glob\(f"{doc_type_dir}/phase-*.md"\)\)
        doc_types[doc_type] = phase_count

for doc_type in sorted\(doc_types.keys\(\)\):
